msgs.text:
Turn 28 — coefficient induction must use the full ghost-kernel, not the q-line alone I continued from the attached problem_statement.md, mega_prompt.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md only. The original problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from the two relators rS,rH, with the required condition πH∘Φ=πS.The new point is a structural correction to the proposed uniform coefficient induction:

Turn 27’s q↦qY4 seed is decisive for the first coefficient layer, but it is not by itself the full induction.It gives the model for the right induction object: not the q-coordinate alone, but the zero-lower-ghost correction kernel together with its square-shadow map.

The standing state before this turn was:The residual Ef,mod2,s=1 Magnus obstruction tower is closed. Turn 18 proves Cm=Lm for every m≥5, so every residual even square quotient is hit by a root transgression and every odd layer is an ordinary boundary.The global compactness framework is available once finite coefficient-level nonemptiness is proved: one must use coefficient-and-degree truncations P/PN,J, not degree-only quotients.The remaining gap is coefficient lifting over the local Ef-coefficient ring

R:=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,m=(2,t).Turns 23–25 rejected square-zero formal smoothness and reframed the coefficient problem as a ghost/Bockstein problem. In particular, after the H1 -change, the dangerous row is(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t),so R/(4,t) sees a 2-Witt ghost and R/(2,t2) sees a t-derivation ghost.

Turn 27 cleared the first A2=R/(4,t) bottleneck: the old Y0↦Y0Y4 seed has nonzero 2-ghost, but the section-cocycle seed q↦qY4 has zero 2-ghost and the same missing B8 square-shadow.The temptation after Turn 27 is to say: “the q-coordinate always supplies the missing ghost-null square shadows.” That is too strong.The reason is simple. A q-correction with initial primitive W is not automatically lower-invisible. Its first residual effect is governed by the same ordinary boundary mechanism that appeared throughout the residual tower; schematically it contributes a term of the form [B,W]in the next Magnus layer. The special seed W=B4 works because[B,B4]=0 in characteristic 2. Thus q↦qY4 is ghost-null and has square shadow B8. But a general W∈Ld does not centralize B. So the q-line alone cannot possibly prove the full coefficient induction.

The correct uniform object is therefore:Kd,r:={coefficient-grade r lower corrections in Y0,Y1,q whose lower coefficient ghost and lower Magnus tail are continuably zero}.

There is a square-shadow mapΘd,r:Kd,r ⟶the next restricted-square obstruction quotient.

Turn 27 proves that, at the first dangerous coefficient bottleneck, this map hits the unique missing line: the zero-2-ghost subspace contains (0,0,e5), i.e. q↦qY4, and its square shadow is (B4)[2]=B8, with nonzero value under the missing functionalλˉ8.

So the correct proposed induction is not:q-seeds alone hit all square shadows.

It is:Θd,r:Kd,r↠the coefficient restricted-square defect quotient for all relevant d,r, where Kd,r includes the combined Y0,Y1,q lower-plus-high torsor.This is a genuine structural reduction. It means higher coefficient layers should not be attacked by expanding the whole relator over arbitrary Artinian quotients. Instead, one should compute the associated graded ghost-kernel map Θd,r. The two coefficient generators 2,t still govern everything, but the relevant freedom is the kernel of their lower ghosts, not a fixed residual lift and not the q-coordinate alone.The t-direction remains less dangerous at this stage. Turn 26 already observed that over At=R/(2,t2), the lower Y0↦Y0Y4 seed is still in the t-ghost kernel, because the first t-ghost attaches to the second coordinate rather than Y0.The genuinely new coefficient phenomenon is the 2-Witt ghost, and Turn 27 shows that the section cocycle supplies at least the first missing zero-2-ghost square shadow.Here is the cleaned-up coefficient induction target.

Let grm

R≃F2 [α,β], with α=in(2),β=in(t).

At the associated graded level,s2+1=2+2t+t2 has initial coefficient α, while s−1=t has initial coefficient β. Thus the coefficient ghost complex is controlled by the regular pair (α,β), together with the residual Magnus boundary. The finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma would follow from the following statement:Coefficient ghost-kernel surjectivity.For every root degree d and coefficient grade r, after imposing the lower ghost-zero conditions and allowing continuation through the already-cleared lower Magnus layers, the square-shadow mapΘd,r:Kd,r→Qd,r hits the full restricted-square defect quotient Qd,r.

Turn 27 proves the first nontrivial instance of this statement at the degree-16 bottleneck. It does not prove the theorem for all d,r. But it does identify the mechanism: the section-cocycle coordinate contributes ghost-null square shadows that are invisible to the lower Y0 -ghost, and these must be included in Kd,r. This is exactly the coefficient analogue of the residual lesson from degrees 10,12,14,16: fixed-gauge tests miss directions that the full lower-plus-high torsor sees.So Turn 28 does not close the final proof. It replaces the vague “promote Turn 27 to an m-adic induction” by a precise rank theorem to prove:

Prove Θd,ris surjective for all d,r.If this rank theorem holds, then every finite coefficient quotient R/J has a solution at every finite Magnus degree. Then the coefficient-and-degree compactness system SN,J is nonempty at every level, and the repaired compactness argument gives the desired over-G isomorphism.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 27’s q↦qY4 seed is not merely an isolated trick: it is the first instance of a broader ghost-null square-shadow mechanism. However, the mechanism must be formulated using the full zero-lower-ghost kernel Kd,r, not the q-coordinate alone. The first A2=R/(4,t) bottleneck is cleared, and the t-ghost remains harmless at that bottleneck.

GAP: The full finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma is still unproved. The remaining precise gap is the surjectivity of the associated graded square-shadow mapsΘd,r:Kd,r↠Qd,r for all root degrees d and coefficient grades r. Without this, I still would not claim a complete proof of ΓS≅GΓH.

NEXT: Compute or prove the first genuinely uniform case of Θd,r: not just the λ-line at d=4, but the full square-shadow image of the zero-2-ghost kernel in the next root layers. The high-value target is to show that the combined Y0,Y1,q kernel, with section-cocycle directions included, maps onto the whole restricted-square quotient, not merely the B8 line.[TURN28-GHOST-KERNEL-INDUCTION-FORMULATED]
